channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: This flashy group of Portland mall walkers puts neon pep into their step
item.description: Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.
item.content:encoded:
Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.

(Image credit: Celeste Noche for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Anti-poverty program is effective even in one of the world's toughest settings
item.description: Researchers looked at whether a tried-and-true poverty alleviation effort that gives cash and coaching to households, and is typically implemented in rural areas, could work in an urban setting.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers looked at whether a tried-and-true poverty alleviation effort that gives cash and coaching to households, and is typically implemented in rural areas, could work in an urban setting.

(Image credit: Ali Ebubekir Tokcan/Anadolu)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change
item.description: The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Here's how medication abortion works with just one drug
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday said that telemedicine access to the abortion drug mifepristone can stay — for at least a week. But as mifepristone faces court challenges, another drug that remains available is safe and effective at ending early pregnancy.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court on Monday said that telemedicine access to the abortion drug mifepristone can stay — for at least a week. But as mifepristone faces court challenges, another drug that remains available is safe and effective at ending early pregnancy.
item.dc:creator: Mara Gordon

item.title: The oldest millennials are 45! This tool helps plan for longevity
item.description: The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.
item.content:encoded:
The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.

(Image credit: Mar Hernández for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: White House says it's cut VA wait times, but new study paints more complicated picture
item.description: The Trump administration claims it has cut VA staff while also shortening wait times for care. A new study shows a more complicated picture, with longer wait times in several key areas.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration claims it has cut VA staff while also shortening wait times for care. A new study shows a more complicated picture, with longer wait times in several key areas.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
item.description: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.
item.content:encoded:
Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: California's rule to add folic acid brings a Hispanic staple into the regulatory fold
item.description: In January, California became the first U.S. state to require food makers to add folic acid to products like tortillas because of the high rate of neural tube defects in Hispanic infants.
item.content:encoded: In January, California became the first U.S. state to require food makers to add folic acid to products like tortillas because of the high rate of neural tube defects in Hispanic infants.
item.dc:creator: Lesley McClurg

item.title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
item.description: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?
item.content:encoded:
Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

(Image credit: Theo Wargo)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
item.description: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang
